gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer Fermat initially found merge blockers around public config helper leakage and dynamic fallback config pickling/slotted behavior. The patch added helper-dependency fingerprinting, a real module-level fallback config class, legacy helper routing, fallback pickling/slotted regression coverage, reran the verification gate, and Fermat approved with no remaining merge blockers.
verification: python -m py_compile src/restaurant_kv_serving/databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py; poetry run pytest tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py::test_legacy_vllm_smoke_job_import_order_does_not_capture_public_monkeypatch tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py::test_legacy_vllm_smoke_job_import_order_ignores_in_place_public_class_mutation tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py::test_legacy_vllm_smoke_job_import_order_ignores_public_config_helper_monkeypatch -q; poetry run pytest tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py -q; poetry run pytest tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q; poetry run pytest -q; git diff --check; poetry check; poetry build; secret scan: rg only matched existing fake credential detector fixtures/evidence text
what_changed: Isolated the legacy Databricks vLLM smoke job facade from pre-import monkeypatches of document_kv_cache.databricks_vllm_smoke_job, including patched constants, replaced config classes, in-place config class mutations, patched public config helpers, and runner writer hooks.
why: The migration-only restaurant_kv_serving facade must preserve legacy compatibility hooks without letting public document namespace test hooks or import-order mutations become legacy defaults. This keeps the Qwen3/vLLM Databricks smoke evidence path stable while implementation ownership continues moving to document_kv_cache.
